purpose: Provide independent server-side acceptance evidence for the complete data and security boundary.
success_outcome: REQ-008 server behavior is reproducibly proven against real Medusa/PostgreSQL, including consumed-source and recovery semantics.
anti_goals: Do not change production merge behavior to make tests pass.
Do not use live providers or production data.
Do not add browser UI coverage.

success_checks: Real Medusa/PostgreSQL proves transfer, merge, ownership, replay, and concurrency.
Consumed source returns Store not-found while journal replay returns target.
Injected post-soft-delete failure restores both carts.
evidence_required: T3 human checkpoint and rollback/recovery evidence.

stop_conditions: Ownership or replay cannot be proven independently of browser UI.
Failure injection cannot prove source and target restoration.
required_handoff: Report changed_files, commands_run, evidence, scope_compliance, and blockers_or_none.
Before T3 closure record exact HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE markers.
